title: Set and Exhibit Designers
description: Design special exhibits and sets for film, video, television, and theater productions. May study scripts, confer with directors, and conduct research to determine appropriate architectural styles.

tasks.text: Develop set designs, based on evaluation of scripts, budgets, research information, and available locations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare rough drafts and scale working drawings of sets, including floor plans, scenery, and properties to be constructed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare preliminary renderings of proposed exhibits, including detailed construction, layout, and material specifications, and diagrams relating to aspects such as special effects or lighting.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read scripts to determine location, set, and design requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Submit plans for approval, and adapt plans to serve intended purposes, or to conform to budget or fabrication restrictions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Attend rehearsals and production meetings to obtain and share information related to sets.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with clients and staff to gather information about exhibit space, proposed themes and content, timelines, budgets, materials, or promotion requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Research architectural and stylistic elements appropriate to the time period to be depicted, consulting experts for information, as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe sets during rehearsals in order to ensure that set elements do not interfere with performance aspects such as cast movement and camera angles.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with those in charge of lighting and sound so that those production aspects can be coordinated with set designs or exhibit layouts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select set props, such as furniture, pictures, lamps, and rugs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design and build scale models of set designs, or miniature sets used in filming backgrounds or special effects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine objects to be included in exhibits to plan where and how to display them.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assign staff to complete design ideas and prepare sketches, illustrations, and detailed drawings of sets, or graphics and animation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect installed exhibits for conformance to specifications and satisfactory operation of special-effects components.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Estimate set- or exhibit-related costs, including materials, construction, and rental of props or locations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan for location-specific issues, such as space limitations, traffic flow patterns, and safety concerns.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Acquire, or arrange for acquisition of, specimens or graphics required to complete exhibits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design and produce displays and materials that can be used to decorate windows, interior displays, or event locations, such as streets and fairgrounds.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Direct and coordinate construction, erection, or decoration activities to ensure that sets or exhibits meet design, budget, and schedule requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Coordinate the transportation of sets that are built off-site, and coordinate their setup at the site of use.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confer with conservators to determine how to handle an exhibit's environmental aspects, such as lighting, temperature, and humidity, so that objects will be protected and exhibits will be enhanced.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and purchase lumber and hardware necessary for set construction.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Arrange for outside contractors to construct exhibit structures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Incorporate security systems into exhibit layouts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate the removal of sets, props, and exhibits after productions or events are complete.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide supportive materials for exhibits and displays, such as press kits, advertising, publicity notices, posters, brochures, catalogues, and invitations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
